New blessings shouldn't overshadow the memory of past blessings, nor should the glory of today’s helpers to the church lessen the rightful honor given to those who came before, since God is the same one who worked through both. Moses gave a very rich and fertile country to part of Israel, but it was outside of Jordan. Joshua gave all of Israel the holy land, within Jordan. So the law has given some of God's spiritual Israel worldly blessings, as guarantees of good things to come; but our Lord Jesus, the true Joshua, provided for all the children of promise spiritual blessings and the heavenly Canaan.

Here we have the boundaries of the country that Joshua conquered. A list is given of the kings who were defeated by Israel: thirty-one in total. This shows how fertile Canaan was at that time, with so many choosing to live together there. This was the land God appointed for Israel; yet today it is one of the most barren and unproductive countries in the world. Such is the effect of the curse it lies under, since its people rejected Christ and his gospel, as Moses foretold. The vengeance of a righteous God, inflicted on all these kings and their subjects for their wickedness, should make us fear and hate sin. The fertile land given to his chosen people should fill our hearts with hope and confidence in his mercy, and with humble gratitude.
